title: Lago
description: Open-source metering and usage-based billing platform for invoicing, subscriptions, and pay-as-you-go pricing.
installation: Lago is a multi-service Rails stack. This template runs the PRIMARY API server (getlago/api) on port 3000, wired to the Postgres and Valkey (Redis) add-ons provisioned here. You MUST deploy the companion services separately, all sharing the SAME database, Redis, and the env keys below: (1) Worker - image getlago/api with command './scripts/start.worker.sh' (Sidekiq background jobs, required for billing/invoicing to function); (2) Clock - image getlago/api with command './scripts/start.clock.sh' (recurring/scheduled jobs); (3) Frontend - image getlago/front on port 80, set its API_URL to this app's public URL and APP_ENV=production; (4) PDF generator - image getlago/lago-gotenberg:7.8.2 on port 3000, point LAGO_PDF_URL at it (default http://lago-pdf:3000). Lago's Postgres needs the partman + uuid-ossp extensions; if the provisioned Postgres lacks them, swap in getlago/postgres-partman:15-alpine. ROTATE before exposing: SECRET_KEY_BASE, LAGO_ENCRYPTION_PRIMARY_KEY, LAGO_ENCRYPTION_DETERMINISTIC_KEY, LAGO_ENCRYPTION_KEY_DERIVATION_SALT (each via 'openssl rand -hex 32'), LAGO_RSA_PRIVATE_KEY (a real PEM via 'openssl genrsa 2048 | base64'), and the Postgres add-on password. First run: the API container runs DB migrations on boot via LAGO_CREATE_ORG; create your first organization/user through the frontend signup. Object storage (S3) for invoice PDFs is optional and not provisioned - configure LAGO_USE_AWS_S3 + bucket env vars if needed.
